message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Madison Club (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: <100 public reviews (private club; Yelp listing is sparse; not on OpenTable). [UNVERIFIED] exact count - Yelp page returned 403
- Sentiment: [UNVERIFIED] - public reviews are minimal and not representative; private club operates outside public review channels. Anecdotal mentions describe high-touch service (caddies, on-course food/beverage); a small number of negative comments exist on third-party listings but volume is too low to characterize meaningfully
- Longevity: Founded ~2007 (built late 2006), opened by Discovery Land Company; ~19 years operating under same developer/operator - stable ownership
- Credentials: []
- Appears in queries: Does NOT surface for public-facing restaurant queries (best restaurants Coachella Valley, fine dining La Quinta, sushi near Palm Springs, date night). Surfaces only for private-club / luxury-real-estate / Discovery Land queries. Appears in goprivategolf.com and real-estate broker directories, not on OpenTable, Yelp dining lists, or Palm Springs Life dining roundups
- Site structured data: madisonclubca.com is a brand/marketing splash site with member login. No schema.org Restaurant or LocalBusiness markup, no published hours, no menu structure, no public location/booking signals an agent could parse. Effectively invisible to dining-intent agents by design
- Visibility gap score: 2
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Extract: any JSON-LD or schema.org markup, any LocalBusiness/Restaurant structured data, published hours, menu structure, public location/booking signals, contact info, and overall site purpose (marketing splash vs full content). Report what's in the HTML source.

input.query: best restaurants Coachella Valley fine dining 2026
fine dining La Quinta best restaurants

content.content:
# The Madison Club - Content Analysis

## Site Purpose
Marketing splash for an exclusive residential community and private club in the Coachella Valley, California.

## Structured Data
No JSON-LD or schema.org markup is present in the provided HTML source.

## Business Type & Contact Info
- **Type**: Private membership community (Discovery Land Company)
- **Phone**: (760) 777-9320
- **Email**: info@madisonclubca.com
- **Location**: Coachella Valley, California

## Key Amenities Listed
The site highlights: "Play golf on a classically designed Tom Fazio course, relax in the members-only clubhouse, or de-stress in the private day spa and fitness facility"

Additional experiences include outdoor pursuits, spa & wellness, and lifestyle activities.

## Hours/Booking
No published hours or public booking system mentioned. Site includes "Members login" link, indicating restricted access.

## Real Estate Component
Dedicated real estate section promoting "luxury estate residences, elegant villas and clubhouse suites" with mountain views.

## Notable Omissions
- No JSON-LD structured data
- No published operating hours
- No public menu or pricing
- No LocalBusiness schema
- Primarily navigation-driven design with weather widget and image galleries

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in La Quinta:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**Morgan's in the Desert**
Morgan's in the Desert is the flagship restaurant at the La Quinta Resort & Club and the undisputed anchor of fine dining in La Quinta. It has been operating since 1926, and features warm lighting, lush courtyard surroundings, commitment to locally sourced ingredients, meticulous plating, and a considered wine list.

**Lavender Bistro**
Lavender Bistro's award-winning patio is strung with thousands of sparkling lights, softened by fresh flowers, and animated by live music. The menu features organic New American cuisine with fresh-caught seafood, fine meats, pastas, and thoughtful vegetarian and vegan options.

**Other Notable Fine Dining Options**

Additional fine dining restaurants in the area include Lola's At The Cove, Kitchen Ten Eleven, La Quinta Cliffhouse, DSRT CLUB, Italica, and Morgan's in the Desert.

Cork & Fork is another consistently praised option, having won more awards than any other restaurant in La Quinta.

Most of these restaurants accept reservations, and several are seasonal, so it's recommended to book in advance during peak dining times.

Morgan's in the Desert, La Quinta Cliffhouse, The Bistro, Oceana Restaurant, Lavender Bistro, Nobu - Indian Wells, and T&T Innovation Kitchen are among the top-rated fine dining establishments.

## Notable Restaurants by Specialty

**Steakhouses:**
The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage specializes in prime steaks and fresh seafood, known for its top-notch service and luxurious setting. The Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and has won the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007.

**French & Italian Cuisine:**
Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list. Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine, offering traditional Italian dishes from homemade pasta to veal marsala.

**Seafood:**
Oceana Restaurant in Palm Desert offers fresh seafood with exquisite flavors and stunning ambiance.

**Farm-to-Table:**
Morgan's in the Desert, located at La Quinta Resort & Club, offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine.

**Contemporary American:**
EIGHT4NINE Restaurant & Lounge in Palm Springs combines modern American cuisine with a chic, contemporary atmosphere, offering everything from classic steaks to innovative vegetarian dishes.

Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week runs May 29 - June 7, 2026, offering special dining experiences throughout the nine-city oasis.

Here's the information about Madison Club in La Quinta based on the search results:

## Overview
Madison Club is a highly prestigious, private, members-only golf community developed by Discovery Land Company, and it is a sophisticated residential community in La Quinta, California that offers a modern interpretation of California's old-line country clubs.

## Opening and Design
The Madison Club golf course was designed by Tom Fazio and opened in 2007. Developer Michael Meldman showed Tom Fazio the land and told him, "Let's do a modern-day Shadow Creek", referring to Fazio's famous Nevada course.

## Community Details
There are several types of residences offered at The Madison Club: luxury estates, elegant villas and clubhouse suites. The club is sought out by CEOs, celebrities, sports figures and other high net worth individuals.

## Membership and Facilities
Private equity membership is limited to 225 property owners. Members can play golf on the classically designed Tom Fazio course, relax in the members-only clubhouse, or de-stress in the private day spa and fitness facility.

Here are the search results for "Madison Club" La Quinta private golf club Discovery Land:

## Overview

The Madison Club is a private golf facility featuring 18 holes of golf in La Quinta, California and was designed by Tom Fazio. This club is being developed by Discovery Land Company, who also developed The Hideaway. The club was founded in 2007.

## Course Details

The 18-hole private par 72 golf course was designed by Tom Fazio in 2007, with back tees measuring 7,426 yards with a 75.40 rating and 143.00 slope.

## Amenities & Facilities

The golf course features design characteristics of the finest traditional private clubs, with stands of mature trees, generous fairways and cascading water – all surrounded by dramatic desert mountain vistas. Additional amenities include world-class spa facilities, a resort style swimming facility, a fitness center, golf performance center, and both fine and casual dining.

## Membership

The club is equity owned with a 225 membership cap. Based on one source, golf membership is $250,000 (limited to property owners only), with annual dues of $33,000.

## Residential Community

There are villas, custom homes, custom home sites and clubhouse suites. The club is located at 53035 Meriwether Way, La Quinta, CA 92253.

## Reservation Platforms

You can book online through OpenTable, a real-time online booking network for fine dining restaurants, where you can read reviews and earn points towards free meals.

## Popular Date Night Restaurants

**La Quinta Cliffhouse**
La Quinta Cliffhouse offers a 2-course menu for $33 from 3:30pm-5pm and requires reservations. The restaurant features classic American cuisine with perfectly executed steak, pasta and fresh seafood, and guests can dine al fresco on the cliffside patio or in the vibrant dining room.

**Lavender Bistro**
This modern Californian restaurant offers pasta, meat, and fish dishes with both indoor and elegant garden patio dining. Reservations for outdoor seating are a must.

**Enzo's Bistro and Bar**
The restaurant serves excellent pasta dishes like bolognese pappardelle and salmon, with reservations recommended for Friday nights.

**Lola's of the Cove**
This is a globally inspired, locally rooted dining destination with thoughtfully crafted plates and refined cocktails in an intimate setting, designed for lingering evenings and shared moments.

For reservations, visit OpenTable's website or individual restaurant websites to book your date night!

Based on the search results, I found information about The Madison Club in La Quinta on Yelp and other platforms:

## Yelp Listing Information

The Madison Club is listed on Yelp as a social club located at 53035 Meriwether Way, La Quinta, CA 92253, with hours 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM. The business is dog-friendly and good for kids. The phone number is (760) 777-9320 and the website is madisonclubca.com.

## About The Madison Club

The Madison Club is a sophisticated residential community in La Quinta, California that offers a modern interpretation of California's old-line country clubs. The facility includes a classically designed Tom Fazio golf course, a members-only clubhouse, and a private day spa and fitness facility.

## Mixed Reviews

The search results show mixed reviews. Some reviewers praised the facility, with one noting "the most amazing day of their golfing life" with bartenders and excellent staff. However, other reviews were more critical, with complaints about service and maintenance issues, including one review stating "Filthy course, terrible service, don't maintain area."

You can visit the Yelp page directly at https://www.yelp.com/biz/the-madison-club-la-quinta to read the full reviews and see the current ratings.

Based on the search results, I found information about The Madison Club in La Quinta, California. Here's what I discovered:

The Madison Club is a sophisticated residential community in La Quinta, California that offers modern interpretation of California's old-line country clubs.

Regarding dining, the Madison clubhouse offers its members exclusive fine dining, craft cocktails, a full-service pool, a refreshment bar, locker rooms, and a gym and spa. The Madison Club's casual luxury and relaxing environment encourages friends and family to enjoy a wide selection of gourmet and traditional delicacies that satisfy every appetite.

The club also features several dining venues, including Madison Place which offers breakfast and lunch snacks, an open bar, and some miso soup, and the Dog House, overlooking holes 9, 18, where there are signature PB&J's, sliders and pressed hot sandwiches.

However, specific detailed menu items beyond these general descriptions are not available in the search results. For a complete current dining menu, you would need to visit their official website directly or contact the club.

content.text: I have enough to verify the claims. One interesting find: there's a separate `madisonclub.org` site with a menus page — let me check briefly.

input.prompt: Is this the same Madison Club in La Quinta CA, or a different club? What does the menus page contain? Any structured data?

content.content:
# Response

This is a **different club** — the webpage is for The Madison Club located in Madison, Wisconsin ("5 East Wilson Street, Madison, WI 53703"), not La Quinta, California.

## Menus Page Content

The menus page primarily contains:

- **Four downloadable menu PDFs**: Lunch Menu, Clementine Dining Menu, Churchill's Lounge Menu, and Saturday Farmers' Market Brunch
- **Photo gallery**: Approximately 70+ images showcasing dining spaces, food, beverages, and club facilities
- **Hours of operation**: Details for the main club and specific dining venues
- **Contact information**: Address, phone number, and email

## Structured Data

The page contains **minimal structured data**. There are no visible schema.org markup, JSON-LD, or other machine-readable metadata beyond standard HTML navigation and document links. The content is primarily presentational (images and PDF links) rather than semantically structured data.

content.text: Confirmed — madisonclub.org is a different club (Wisconsin), not the La Quinta entity. All checks complete.

confirms: Site structured data: madisonclubca.com is a marketing splash with members login; no JSON-LD, no schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness, no published hours, no menu structure, no public booking signals — verified by fetching the homepage

Does NOT surface for public-facing restaurant queries — verified by running 'best restaurants Coachella Valley fine dining 2026', 'fine dining La Quinta best restaurants', and 'date night La Quinta restaurants reservations'; Madison Club appeared in none of them. Top results consistently cite Morgan's in the Desert, Lavender Bistro, La Quinta Cliffhouse, Lola's at the Cove, etc.

Surfaces only for private-club / luxury-real-estate / Discovery Land queries — confirmed; results were golfday.us, planetgolf.com, realestateranchomirage.com, bdhomes.com, laquintaluxuryrealty.com, GolfPass, GolfDigest
Founded ~2007 by Discovery Land Company / Tom Fazio course design — multiple independent sources (GolfDigest, GolfPass, golfday.us, Planet Golf) confirm 2007 opening and Discovery Land developer
Not on OpenTable — OpenTable La Quinta fine-dining and date-night lists do not include Madison Club

Yelp listing is sparse and categorized as 'Social Clubs' (not Restaurants); reviews are mixed/minimal with both praise and complaints — consistent with claim of low review volume and anecdotal sentiment
contradicts: Minor: Yelp listing does publish hours (7:00 AM - 6:00 PM) per search snippet — the claim 'no published hours' applies to madisonclubca.com (correct) but third-party Yelp does surface hours, so an agent crawling Yelp could find them. Does not materially change the visibility-gap assessment.
flagged_as_unverified: Exact public review count <100 — Yelp page snippet mentions '10 Photos' but did not return a numeric review count in search results; directionally plausible (sparse) but exact number not independently confirmed
Specific claim that club was 'built late 2006' — sources confirm 2007 opening but construction-start date not independently verified
Same developer/operator for ~19 years (stable ownership) — Discovery Land Company association is confirmed but continuous unchanged ownership over the full period not independently audited

Visibility gap score of 2 and convertibility tier 'anchor-reference' — these are framework-internal scores, not externally verifiable, but the underlying evidence (no dining-query surfacing, no schema, no OpenTable) is consistent with a very low visibility score
input.notes: Core assessment holds strongly. The Madison Club is effectively invisible to dining-intent agents by design: madisonclubca.com is a brand splash with no schema.org markup, no menu, no public hours, no booking surface. It does not appear in any of the four canonical dining queries (best restaurants CV, fine dining La Quinta, date night La Quinta, fine dining 2026) — those are dominated by Morgan's in the Desert, Lavender Bistro, Cliffhouse, Lola's at the Cove. It does surface for golf/real-estate/Discovery Land queries as claimed. Yelp lists it as 'Social Clubs', not a restaurant, with sparse reviews. One small caveat: Yelp does publish 7am-6pm hours, so the 'no published hours' framing should be scoped to the entity's own site, not the broader web. Anchor-reference tier is well-supported.
